title: Support for open and close ProcScriptStatements
breadcrumbs: Support for open and close ProcScriptStatements
text: Support for open and close ProcScriptStatements Most DBMS connectors fully support the open and close ProcScript instructions. The open instruction opens the relevant path and attempts to log on to the database. The close instruction closes all tables and logs off from the database, shutting down that path. Note: The open and close statements are not supported for the TXT and SEQ connectors. Related Links open Open the specified DBMS or path, or connect to the Uniface Router. close Log off from the specified path or from all paths. Database Access Uniface automatically opens the DBMS when a read or write ProcScript statement is issued. The path and logon details for the specific database (or databases) can be provided in an assignment file, a command line instruction, or in the open ProcScript instruction.
